description.en: This ontology includes medical devices, catalogued within the regulatory frameworks European MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746). Its purpose is to represent medical devices, their manufacturers, risks, incidents and corrective actions.

comments.en: Ontology for incidents, risk and actions related to medical devices.

comment.en: The regulatory classification assigned to a medical device based on its intended purpose and inherent risks, determining the level of oversight required.
A set of laws.
Risk associated with vulnerabilities in the network or software of a medical device that could lead to unauthorized access or malfunction.
A medical device.
Risk that the device's function is disrupted by external factors, such as electromagnetic fields.
A specific risk category that measures the direct negative impact on a patient's health status.
Combination of the probability of occurrence of harm and the severity of that harm.
A physical location.
The set of rules, laws, and regulations that govern the manufacturing, registration, and safety monitoring of medical devices within a specific jurisdiction, such as the European Union.
A corrective action performed on the device at the location where it is used, without the need for its return to the manufacturer.
An event happening at a certain time and location.
Category of risk related to manufacturing defects, packaging failures, or lack of batch consistency.
Risk associated with the emission of ionizing or non-ionizing radiation from the medical device.
A public or private organization.
Device to be used at home
A predefined qualitative value for a characteristic.
Risk that the device does not operate as intended due to internal technical defects or software errors.
Individual representing the patient harm risk category.
General classification of risk types, such as device performance, safety, or patient harm.
The specific status of an action.

label.en: IVDR (In Vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices Regulation)

comment.en: Regulation (EU) 2017/746 of the European Parliament and of the Council on in vitro diagnostic medical devices and repealing Directive 98/79/EC and Commission Decision 2010/227/EU.
Individual representing a closed status.
Individual representing a open status.

comment.undefined: Current state of a Field Safety Corrective Action, such as Open, Closed, or Pending.

comment.en: Instance representing Health Products Regulatory Authority.
The national body responsible for the registration and supervision of medical devices and manufacturers within its jurisdiction.
A category of risk where the device fails to perform its intended function or meet its specifications.
High individual risk and/or moderate public health risk in vitro diagnostic devices, such as those used for genetic testing or cancer screening.
High individual risk and high public health risk in vitro diagnostic devices, typically used for detecting life-threatening infectious diseases in blood supply.
Risk related to software bugs, algorithm failures, or data processing errors in active devices.
Risk associated with the materials used in the device, including toxicity, degradation, or biocompatibility issues.

description.undefined: Thrombus formation on device surface causing embolic events

comment.en: Instance of DEV-001 device
Device that can be inserted inside the human body.
Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council on medical devices, amending Directive 2001/83/EC, Regulation (EC) No 178/2002 and Regulation (EC) No 1223/2009.
An active device that is also implantable.
Any measure aimed at achieving the return of a device that has already been made available to the end user.
Instance representing the manufacturer MAN-001.
Natural or legal person who manufactures or fully refurbishes a device and markets it under their name or trademark.
Individual representing a low probability level.
The likelihood of a specific risk occurring during the use of a medical device.
An action performed by an individual.
Risk category focusing on the potential for the device to cause physical harm or injury during normal use.
A device used in vitro for the examination of specimens.
Risk related to incorrect or delayed results from diagnostic devices that may lead to inappropriate medical decisions.
Risk arising from incorrect or misleading diagnostic information provided by the device.
Corrective action taken by a manufacturer for technical or medical reasons to prevent or reduce the risk of a serious incident.
Medium-high risk medical devices, often including more complex implants or long-term invasive devices, subject to stricter assessment.
Medium-risk medical devices, including some diagnostic and therapeutic tools, that require a moderate level of monitoring and certification.
The device can only be used one time.
Any offered product, service, or manufactured good.

description.undefined: Patient implanted with HeartMate 3 LVAD developed ischemic stroke 6 months post-implant. Device thrombus confirmed by echocardiography.

comment.en: Instance representing the INC-001 incident.
Any malfunction or deterioration in the characteristics or performance of a device made available on the market.e
High-risk medical devices, typically those that support or sustain life, are implanted, or present a potential significant risk of illness or injury.
Instance representing the country Ireland.
Instance representing the country Germany.
A distinct nation with its own government.
Individual representing a critical severity level.
A measure of the possible consequences of a hazard or a specific risk.
Risk related to the effectiveness or functional output of the device compared to its intended use.
Risk of biological, chemical, or particulate contamination that may affect the safety or performance of the device.

description.undefined: Safety alert issued to all implanting centres regarding anticoagulation protocol update for HeartMate 3 to reduce device thrombosis risk. INR target range revised to 2.0–3.0.

comment.en: Instance representing the Field Safety Corrective Action 001.
An alert warning a safety hazard.
Instance representing the risk RSK-003.
Risk of physical trauma or damage to the human body associated with device operation or failure.
A device intended to be totally introduced into the human body.
Instance representing the European Union region.
A geographical region under the jurisdiction of a government.
Communication sent by a manufacturer to users or customers in relation to a Field Safety Corrective Action.
Risk of clinical infection to the patient or user resulting from the use or contamination of the device.
A device that relies on a source of electrical energy.
The name of the risk.
The date when the device malfunction was detected.
Links a safety incident or a corrective action to the physical medical device involved in the event.
A name for an entity.
The authorization number assigned to the manufacturer.
Tells if the device is intended to be reused.
The number assigned to the manufacturer in the EUDAMED database.
Relates a Field Safety Corrective Action (FSCA) to the specific countries where the action is being implemented.
Classifies a Risk into a specific functional category, such as Device Failure or Patient Harm.
Tells if the device is intended for in vitro diagnostic.
Associates a medical device with a potential hazard or risk identified during its lifecycle.
The specific country where an incident was officially identified and reported to the authorities.
The date when the Field Safety Corrective Action was closed.
Links a specific Risk to its estimated likelihood of occurrence (e.g., Low, Medium, High).
A identifier for the resource.
A device identifier.
Number of devices affected by an incident.
Tells if the device depends on a source of power.
The date when the Field Safety Corrective Action was initiated or performed.
Relates a Field Safety Corrective Action (FSCA) to the specific incident it aims to mitigate or resolve.
Associates a medical device with its applicable legal framework, such as MDR or IVDR.
Relates a risk or incident to the specific category or type of medical device that is impacted.
The date when the incident occurred.
Tells if a corrective action has been implemented.
Relates a medical device or manufacturer to the specific regulatory framework (MDR/IVDR) it must comply with.
Relates a medical device or manufacturer to the specific regulatory framework (MDR/IVDR) it must comply with.
Relates an Incident to the manufacturer or entity responsible for submitting the official safety report.
Indicates the current stage of a Field Safety Corrective Action, such as Open, Closed, or Pending.
The age of the patient.
The date when the incident was reported.
Links a manufacturer or an incident report to the specific country or geographical region involved.
The range actions in a Field Safety Corrective Action.
A general date.
Indicates the current stage of a Field Safety Corrective Action, such as Open, Closed, or Pending.
The relationship between a medical device and the legal entity responsible for its design and production.
The use intended for a device.
Relates a Risk to the magnitude of its potential consequences or impact on health.
A descriptive text explaining the resource
The sex of the patient.
Tells if the device is intended to be into the human body.
A email address.
The result of an incident.
Connects a device to its regulatory risk classification.
Relates a medical device or manufacturer to the specific regulatory framework (MDR/IVDR) it must comply with.
